| 11 | Final adoption gate: ship only if deterministic geometry, contrast, casing/glyph, palette, and MiniMax status pass. Otherwise keep prior best or downgrade. | A1 to A7; all RCs | M | all tiles |

## 2. PREDICTED LIFT

| Scenario | SHIP rate | Diagram-vs-linear delta | Interpretation |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Baseline | `27/45 = 60%` | `~41 pts` | Linear tiles already strong; 2D-positioned diagrams are the core defect. |
| First move only: A1 prompt + deterministic gate + one repair | `34-37/45 = 76-82%` | `15-22 pts` | Best lift-per-effort. Converts many overflow/title/contrast failures and forces risky diagrams into safer primitives. |
| Conservative full pipeline | `36-38/45 = 80-84%` | `14-20 pts` | Assumes overlap among A1/A3/A4/A5 gains and some remaining aesthetic/slop failures. |
| Optimistic full pipeline | `39-41/45 = 87-91%` | `8-14 pts` | Requires skeleton-first 2D renderer to work on most matrix/node/funnel failures without damaging linear winners. |

| Cause | Expected effect |
|---|---|

| Linear-flow tiles already score `86-94`. | The program must preserve them, not over-plan them. |
| A5/A7 skeleton-first should recover additional dense 2D holdouts after A1. | Adds roughly `+2` to `+4` tiles beyond first move if renderer/scaffold is implemented well. |
| A4 repair consumes already-created MiniMax findings instead of discarding them. | Adds `+1` to `+3` net tiles after overlap, mainly contrast/palette/clipping. |
| IFScale warns that dense instructions are omitted. | Keep prompt contract short and move checks into validators. |

Contrast expectation: accepted outputs should reach `95-100%` contrast exit-0 because dark text tokens and actual CSS contrast checks are hard gates.

| Item | Verdict | RC-id(s) | Predicted contribution | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 safe linear contract | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3`, `RC-4`, `RC-5` | `+7` to `+10` SHIP tiles when paired with gate/repair | Highest direct match to defect: bounded visual box, title band, primitive caps, banned gray tokens. |
| A2 reasoning-embedded rubric | ADAPT | `RC-2`, `RC-5`, `RC-6`, `RC-7` | `+2` to `+5` tiles, mostly through A1 synergy | Keep the JSON preflight and anti-slop semantics, but do not add a long rubric wall. |
| A3 primitive-routed repair | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3`, `RC-8` | `+3` to `+6` incremental after prompt gate | Strong for route-specific repair. Use one failure-triggered turn, not always-on self-refine. |
| A4 MiniMax issue-to-repair adapter | ADOPT | `RC-1` to `RC-6`, `RC-8` | `+1` to `+3` incremental after A1/A3 overlap | Turns free-text audit into typed repair objects and prevents false-fixes with adopt-if gates. |
| A5 skeleton-first 2D | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3` | `+2` to `+4` incremental after A1; larger if many 2D holdouts remain | Correct long-term mechanism: upstream geometry owns boxes, GLM renders. |
| A6 GPT-5.5 skeleton author | ADAPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3`, `RC-4` | `+1` to `+3` on hardest 2D cases | Use only for dense/high-risk 2D or after skeleton failure. Too expensive for linear tiles. |
| A7 Bento Geometry Kernel | ADAPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3`, `RC-5`, `RC-7` | Long-term ceiling raiser to `39-41/45` | Correct architecture, but bigger engineering lift. Implement after A1 gate proves ROI. |
| LaTCoder, `https://arxiv.org/html/2508.03560v1` | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3` | High | Supports bounded regions, block-wise synthesis, and layout-preserved assembly. |
| IFScale, `https://arxiv.org/html/2507.11538v1` | ADOPT | `RC-3`, `RC-4`, `RC-5` | High | Explains why long prompt prose fails. Put hard rules early and validate mechanically. |
| LaySPA, `https://arxiv.org/html/2509.16891v2` | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2` | High | Supports explicit layout objects and collision/alignment/spacing rewards. |
| GeoSVG-RL, `https://arxiv.org/html/2605.25447` | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3` | High | Supports plan-first generation plus browser-backed geometry extraction. |
| Self-Refine, `https://github.com/madaan/self-refine` | ADAPT | `RC-8`, secondary `RC-1` to `RC-5` | Medium | Use feedback/refine once after measured failure. Do not run open-ended self-refine. |
| Visual critique refinement, `https://arxiv.org/html/2412.16829` | ADOPT | `RC-2`, `RC-5`, `RC-8` | Medium | Supports localized issue evidence, bboxes, and refinement validation. |
| Chain-of-Rubrics | ADAPT | `RC-4`, `RC-5`, `RC-6`, `RC-7` | Medium | Useful as short gate sequence, harmful if expanded into dense prose. |
| DesignerlyLoop, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.15331` | ADAPT | `RC-6`, `RC-7` | Low to medium | Supports externalized planning and curated examples, but geometry still needs validators. |
| UI/UX LLM SLR, `https://arxiv.org/html/2507.04469v1` | ADAPT | `RC-6`, `RC-7` | Low to medium | Supports structured prompting and evaluation; also warns prompt-only spatial reasoning is weak. |
| DesignersForest semantic-token guidance | ADOPT | `RC-5`, `RC-6`, `RC-7` | Medium | Semantic color tokens and auto-layout defaults map directly to contrast and anti-slop failures. |
| GLM-5.2 / GLM-5V docs | ADAPT | `RC-8` | Medium operational risk reduction | Use direct Z.AI multimodal path where proven. Do not trust GLM self-audit as acceptance source. |
| AdaCoder, `https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2504.04220` | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-8` | Medium cost control | Supports adaptive planning: escalate only when the verifier proves native generation failed. |
| DiagramEval, `https://arxiv.org/html/2510.25761` | ADAPT | `RC-2`, `RC-7` | Medium for later kernel | Useful for graph/node/edge agreement checks once diagrams are rendered from semantic plans. |
| DCGen, `https://arxiv.org/html/2406.16386v1` | ADOPT | `RC-1`, `RC-3` | Medium | Reinforces region ownership and decomposition for design-to-code layout. |

## 4. HIGHEST-ROI FIRST MOVE

Ship **A1 prompt replacement + deterministic DOM/CSS gate + one failure-only repair** first.

Why this dominates lift-per-effort:

| Factor | Reason |
|---|---|
| Highest defect coverage | Directly hits `RC-1`, `RC-2`, `RC-3`, `RC-4`, and `RC-5`, which are the recurring hard failures. |
| Low implementation cost | Mostly prompt block replacement plus a browser bbox/contrast gate. No deterministic renderer or GPT-5.5 skeleton service required. |
| Applies to all tiles | Protects linear winners while forcing risky diagrams away from freeform positioning. |
| Non-additive proof point | It creates the measurement surface needed for A3/A4/A5: failure JSON, bboxes, title-zone intrusion, contrast, clipping. |
| Expected lift | Best first experiment target is `34-37/45 SHIP`, from `60%` to `76-82%`, with diagram gap reduced to `15-22 pts`. |

21: |---|---|
22: | A2 rubric expansion | GLM-5.2 profile favors lean COSTAR prompting. Long rubric prose risks omission and instruction dilution. Keep metadata short and machine-checked. |
23: | A4 MiniMax repair adapter | Useful only if MiniMax findings have high precision. Otherwise it adds paid calls and repair churn after deterministic pass already handled obvious failures. |
24: | A6 GPT-5.5 skeleton author | Likely too expensive as default. Use only when deterministic skeleton/ranker fails on high-value 2D tiles. |
25: | A7 full Bento Geometry Kernel | Correct architecture, but high engineering cost. It can become a custom renderer project before ROI is proven. |
26: | Literature-backed lift estimates | Papers support mechanisms, not your tile-specific delta. They justify experiments, not `+N` shipped tiles. |
27: | One repair pass for 2D layouts | Good for local fixes, weak for fundamentally overcrowded geometry. Dense node/matrix/funnel failures need replanning, not patching. |
28: | A1 safe linear contract | High pass-rate lift, but may reduce visual richness. It can win by avoiding hard layouts rather than improving them. |
29: 
30: **3. Cost Ceiling**
31: Assuming GLM dispatch cost is `0` under the Z.AI plan, the real costs are quota, latency, and paid escalation calls.
32: 
33: | Tile Path | Paid Calls | GLM Calls | Expected Wall-Clock |
34: |---|---:|---:|---|
35: | Linear pass first try | `0` | `1` | `~30-45s` typical including render/gate |
36: | Linear fail + one GLM repair | `0` | `2` | `~60-120s` typical, slow tail can hit `3-6 min` |

40: | MiniMax visual audit added | `+1 MiniMax` | unchanged | add one paid vision/audit latency per audited tile |
41: 
42: Batch ceiling for 45 tiles:
43: 
44: | Scope | Ceiling |
45: |---|---|
46: | A1 + gate + one repair, using baseline `18` failures | `45 + 18 = 63` GLM calls, `0` paid calls |
47: | MiniMax on every tile | `45` paid MiniMax calls, likely unnecessary |
48: | MiniMax failure-only on baseline failures | up to `18` paid MiniMax calls |
49: | GPT-5.5 skeleton on all 2D failures | `N_2D * 1-4` paid GPT-5.5 calls |
50: | Worst paid escalation if every failing tile is high-risk 2D | up to `18 * 4 = 72` GPT-5.5 calls plus up to `18` MiniMax calls |
51: 
52: Exact dollar ceiling is `UNKNOWN` without live token prices and prompt/image sizes. Track ceiling as `paid calls × avg input/output/image tokens × provider price`.
53: 
54: **4. Pilot First**
55: Pilot in this order:
56: 
57: | Pilot Step | Goal |
58: |---|---|
59: | Offline validator calibration on existing 45 renders | Measure false positives/false negatives before spending model calls. |
60: | A1 prompt + deterministic gate + one GLM repair on a stratified sample | Use 12-15 tiles: linear winners, known 2D failures, borderline contrast/title failures. |
61: | Shadow MiniMax audit only | Run MiniMax but do not gate on it. Compare precision against human labels. |
62: | Skeleton-first only on residual 2D failures | Test deterministic skeleton quality before adding GPT-5.5. |
63: | GPT-5.5 skeleton author only as A/B escalation | Compare against deterministic skeletons on the same failed 2D cases. |
64: 
65: Go/no-go metrics:
66: 
67: | Metric | Suggested Bar |
68: |---|---|
69: | Linear no-regression | `>=90%` of prior linear winners still pass and remain semantically equivalent |
70: | Validator precision | `<15%` human-disagreed blocks |
71: | Repair safety | `0` copy/casing/glyph regressions on locked fields |
72: | 2D recovery | at least `+3` recovered tiles in the pilot sample before building A7 |
73: | Cost | no paid calls in first pilot except shadow MiniMax sample |
74: | Latency | median per tile under `90s`; slow-tail policy explicit |
75: 
76: **5. Over-Optimistic Lift Assumptions**
77: | Claim | Concern |
78: |---|---|
79: | `34-37/45` from first move | Plausible, but only if “ship” allows safer downgrades. If preserving diagram richness matters, expect lower lift. |
80: | `39-41/45` optimistic full pipeline | Aggressive for `n=45`. One tile is `2.2 pts`, so `+1` to `+3` tile claims are noisy. Use paired tile-level deltas. |
81: | A1/A3/A4/A5 contributions | Strongly overlapping. Do not add gains from prompt, repair, skeleton, and audit independently. |
82: | Skeleton-first `+2` to `+4` | Assumes skeleton scoring predicts real rendered quality and GLM follows the skeleton. Both need proof. |
83: | MiniMax `+1` to `+3` | Only true if its findings are precise and repairable. Otherwise net effect can be zero or negative. |
84: | `95-100%` contrast exit-0 | This is mostly tautological if contrast is a hard gate. It proves accepted outputs pass contrast, not that the system shipped more good tiles. |
85: | Literature transfer | The cited systems support the architecture, but they do not validate GLM-5.2 on your tile distribution. |
86: 
87: Best first move remains A1 + deterministic gate + one repair, but define success as paired quality lift with semantic preservation, not just a higher `SHIP` count.

72: ## 2. PROBLEM & PURPOSE
73: 
74: ### Problem Statement
75: The deterministic geometry skeleton (phase 004) recovers many 2D-positioned tiles, but the hardest ones — dense matrix / node-diagram / branch / connector-map / popover tiles — still fail the phase-001 spatial gate (RC-1 overflow, RC-2 node collisions, RC-3 title-zone break) even after the best-of-3 deterministic recompute. For those residual high-value 2D tiles there is no path between "deterministic skeleton failed" and "downgrade to a linear primitive", so genuinely diagram-shaped content is lost rather than recovered. Linear-flow tiles already score 86-94 and need no help.
76: 
77: ### Purpose
78: Add a cost-capped escalation that hands the hardest residual 2D-risk tiles to GPT-5.5 as a geometry-skeleton author: it computes a coordinate/safe-zone skeleton (the same JSON schema phase 004 emits) that phase 004 then compiles to a locked scaffold for GLM-5.2 to render — recovering diagram tiles that the deterministic skeleton cannot, without spending a paid model on linear tiles that are already strong.
79: 
80: > Grounding (cite RC-ids): A6 final recommendation `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r2-A6.md`; pipeline step 8 escalation `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r4-pipeline.md`; cost ceiling `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r4-risk.md`; dispatch contract `.opencode/skills/cli-opencode/SKILL.md`.
81: <!-- /ANCHOR:problem -->

220: <!-- ANCHOR:summary -->
221: ## 1. SUMMARY
222: 
223: ### Technical Context
224: 
225: | Aspect | Value |
226: |--------|-------|
227: | **Language/Stack** | Node.js ESM (the bento generation harness, `gen-tile.mjs`) |
228: | **Framework** | None - file-emitting CLI harness; renderer is GLM-5.2 |
229: | **Storage** | None - tile artifacts + per-tile skeleton/gate JSON on disk |
230: | **Testing** | Phase-001 deterministic gate + a score-lift-per-dollar A/B holdout harness |
231: | **Escalation model** | GPT-5.5-fast via cli-opencode (`openai` provider, paid) |
232: | **Renderer model** | GLM-5.2 via Z.AI Coding Plan (subscription, dispatch cost 0) |
233: 
234: ### Overview
235: When the deterministic skeleton path (phase 004, including its best-of-3 recompute) fails the phase-001 spatial gate on a high-risk 2D tile, dispatch GPT-5.5-fast at `--variant xhigh` to author a fresh coordinate/safe-zone skeleton in the existing phase-004 JSON schema. Phase 004 compiles that skeleton into the same locked HTML/CSS scaffold and GLM-5.2 renders it; the phase-001 gate re-runs. The branch is cost-capped (`<=1` call and `<=4200` tokens per tile, batch budget gated on the pre-spend `N_2D` count) and measured per GPT-5.5 dollar against deterministic-skeleton-only.

256: <!-- ANCHOR:architecture -->
257: ## 3. ARCHITECTURE
258: 
259: ### Pattern
260: Escalation provider behind a deterministic default (strategy + failure-triggered fallback). The deterministic skeleton author stays the default; GPT-5.5 is the escalation author, fired only after deterministic failure on a qualifying tile (AdaCoder adaptive-planning principle, iter-r2-A6).
261: 
262: ### Key Components
263: - **EscalationClassifier**: applies `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` to the tile's primitive, item count, connector needs, and the phase-004 failure state; emits a per-tile decision + reason.
264: - **Gpt55SkeletonAuthor**: builds the skeleton-author prompt, runs the cli-opencode dispatch, and validates the returned JSON against the phase-004 schema.
265: - **CostCapAccountant**: enforces `<=1` call and `<=4200` tokens per tile and the batch budget derived from the pre-spend `N_2D`; refuses + logs over-cap calls.
266: - **SkeletonAuthorRouter**: in `gen-tile.mjs`, routes the 2D skeleton step deterministic-author -> (on failure + escalate) GPT-5.5-author -> (on failure) downgrade-to-linear (phase 004).
267: 
268: ### Data Flow
269: 1. Phase 002 labels the tile 2D-positioned; phase 004 computes the deterministic skeleton; GLM renders; phase 001 gate runs.
270: 2. On gate failure, phase 004 best-of-3 recompute runs; if it still fails, control reaches the EscalationClassifier.
271: 3. If `trigger_gpt55_if` holds and `skip_gpt55_if` does not, and the batch budget allows, dispatch GPT-5.5 to author a skeleton.
272: 4. Validate the JSON skeleton -> hand to the unchanged phase-004 compile + GLM-render path -> re-run the phase-001 gate.
273: 5. Pass -> SHIP; fail -> downgrade-to-linear (phase 004). Either way the CostCapAccountant records the spend.
274: 
275: ### Exact dispatch (cli-opencode)
276: Dispatched from the bento generation harness (a Node process), which is the legitimate external-dispatch use case - not an OpenCode self-invocation. If the harness ever runs inside OpenCode, route the openai dispatch through a sibling cli-* / fresh shell per the cli-opencode self-invocation guard.
277: 
278: ```bash
279: # Provider auth pre-flight first (once per run): require openai; never substitute silently.
280: PROVIDERS=$(opencode providers list 2>&1)
281: echo "$PROVIDERS" | grep -q "openai" && OPENAI_OK=1 || OPENAI_OK=0
282: # OPENAI_OK=0 -> disable escalation, fall back to deterministic skeleton + downgrade-to-linear, ASK operator.
283: # Confirm the live slug before first dispatch: opencode models openai
284: 
285: opencode run \
286:   --model openai/gpt-5.5-fast \
287:   --variant xhigh \
288:   --format json \
289:   --dir "$REPO_ROOT" \
290:   "$SKELETON_AUTHOR_PROMPT" </dev/null
291: # Notes:
292: #   - openai/gpt-5.5-fast = low-latency Fast variant (OpenAI priority service tier); paid premium provider.
293: #   - --variant xhigh = max reasoning effort (gpt-5.5 effort range tops out at xhigh).
294: #   - omit --agent (top-level --agent is rejected); the author role is stated in the prompt body.
295: #   - </dev/null gives stdin EOF so a non-interactive run does not hang.
296: ```
297: 
298: ### Skeleton-author prompt (geometry only; returns JSON, never tile code)
299: Faithful to iter-r2-A6; the canvas contract + coordinate system are the locked phase-004 zones.
300: 
301: ```text
302: You are the geometry skeleton author for a 480px-high maritime B2B dashboard bento tile.
303: You do not write final UI code. You produce a prevalidated JSON geometry skeleton that will be
304: compiled into locked HTML/CSS for GLM-5.2 to render.
305: 
306: Hard canvas contract:
307: - Tile height is 480px.
308: - diagramFrame = { x: 24, y: 24, w: "calc(100% - 48px)", h: 312, maxY: 336 }.
309: - titleFrame   = { x: 24, y: 368, w: "calc(100% - 48px)", h: 88 }.
310: - All diagram content fits inside diagramFrame; nothing may enter y=336..368; title renders bottom-left in titleFrame.
311: 
312: Coordinate system:
313: - BBoxes are relative to diagramFrame. x and w use 0..1000 horizontal units scaled to diagramFrame width; y and h are pixels 0..312.
314: - A bbox is valid only if x >= 0, y >= 0, x + w <= 1000, y + h <= 312.
315: 
316: Diagram constraints:
317: - Min node gap 16px; min text-to-node gap 12px; at most 4 visible rows (collapse remainder to "+N more"); row height 36-42px; row gap 6-8px.
318: - Edge labels and status pills get their own bbox and may not overlap nodes; connectors use explicit anchors (top/right/bottom/left/center); prefer orthogonal.
319: - Eyebrow is Title Case; avoid CSS text-transform uppercase; use the intended anchor glyph only.
320: 
321: Return JSON only. No prose.
322: ```
323: 
324: The returned object is the phase-004 skeleton schema: `layoutPrimitive`, `riskFlags`, `zones {diagramFrame, titleFrame}`, `coordinateSystem`, `nodes[]`, `rows[]`, `edges[]`, `overflowPlan`, `textRules`, `validation`.
325: 
326: ### Escalation trigger + cost cap
327: Source: iter-r2-A6 `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` / `cost_caps`; batch ceiling from iter-r4-risk.
328: 
329: ```yaml
330: escalation:
331:   # Precondition: tile is 2D-positioned AND the deterministic skeleton path (incl. best-of-3) failed the phase-001 gate.
332:   trigger_gpt55_if:
333:     - layoutPrimitive in [matrix, flow, hub, branch, popover, node-diagram, network, connector-map]
334:     - itemCount > 4 and layoutPrimitive not in [linear, stack, simple-kpi, text-card, timeline]
335:     - brief requires cross-links, connector lines, branching, overlays, pills, edge labels, or popovers
336:   skip_gpt55_if:
337:     - layoutPrimitive in [linear, stack, simple-kpi, text-card, timeline]
338:     - no connector geometry
339:     - no more than 4 visible rows
340:     - tile already passes the phase-001 gate
341: 
342: cost_caps:
343:   max_gpt55_calls_per_2d_tile: 1
344:   max_gpt55_tokens_per_2d_tile: 4200
345:   no_gpt55_for_linear_tiles: true
346:   # Batch: report N_2D (qualifying failed tiles) BEFORE spend; abort batch if projected paid calls exceed N_2D.
347:   # GLM render cost = 0 (Z.AI Coding Plan subscription); GPT-5.5 (openai) is paid.
348:   # Worst case if all ~18 baseline failures are high-risk 2D: <=18 GPT-5.5 calls, <=75.6K tokens (18 x 4.2K).
349:   # Exact dollar ceiling is UNKNOWN without live token prices: track paid_calls x avg_tokens x provider_price.
350: ```

<!-- ANCHOR:problem -->
## 2. PROBLEM & PURPOSE

### Problem Statement
The deterministic geometry skeleton (phase 004) recovers many 2D-positioned tiles, but the hardest ones — dense matrix / node-diagram / branch / connector-map / popover tiles — still fail the phase-001 spatial gate (RC-1 overflow, RC-2 node collisions, RC-3 title-zone break) even after the best-of-3 deterministic recompute. For those residual high-value 2D tiles there is no path between "deterministic skeleton failed" and "downgrade to a linear primitive", so genuinely diagram-shaped content is lost rather than recovered. Linear-flow tiles already score 86-94 and need no help.

### Purpose
Add a cost-capped escalation that hands the hardest residual 2D-risk tiles to GPT-5.5 as a geometry-skeleton author: it computes a coordinate/safe-zone skeleton (the same JSON schema phase 004 emits) that phase 004 then compiles to a locked scaffold for GLM-5.2 to render — recovering diagram tiles that the deterministic skeleton cannot, without spending a paid model on linear tiles that are already strong.

> Grounding (cite RC-ids): A6 final recommendation `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r2-A6.md`; pipeline step 8 escalation `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r4-pipeline.md`; cost ceiling `../../004-bento-visuals/research/iterations/iter-r4-risk.md`; dispatch contract `.opencode/skills/cli-opencode/SKILL.md`.
<!-- /ANCHOR:problem -->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:scope -->
## 3. SCOPE

### In Scope
- An escalation branch in the phase-004 skeleton service that authors a 2D geometry skeleton with GPT-5.5 when, and only when, the deterministic skeleton path has failed on a high-risk 2D tile.
- The escalation trigger rule (`trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if`) and a hard per-tile + per-batch cost cap.
- The GPT-5.5 dispatch via cli-opencode `opencode run --model openai/gpt-5.5-fast --variant xhigh`, with provider auth pre-flight.
- A score-lift-per-dollar A/B measurement on 2D holdouts (GPT-5.5 skeleton vs deterministic-skeleton-only).

### Out of Scope
- Final tile HTML/CSS authoring - GPT-5.5 returns a JSON geometry skeleton only; GLM-5.2 stays the renderer.
- Linear tiles (table / timeline / list / donut / simple-kpi / text-card) - they already score 86-94, so expected lift is ~0 and they are never escalated.
- The deterministic skeleton compute, best-of-3, and downgrade-to-linear - owned by phase 004.
- The spatial contract and deterministic gate - owned by phase 001.
- Any code in this planning pass (planning only - no implementation yet).

### Files to Change
Concrete surface; deterministic-skeleton internals remain phase-004-owned.

| File Path | Change Type | Description |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| `.opencode/specs/anobel.com/004-bento-visuals/scratch/faithful-import/dist/gen-tile.mjs` | Modify | Wire the GPT-5.5 escalation branch into the 2D skeleton step (behind the deterministic author) |
| skeleton service (phase-004-owned, new) | Modify | Add a `gpt55-skeleton-author` provider behind the deterministic skeleton author |
| escalation classifier + cost-cap accountant (new) | Create | `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` decision + per-tile and batch budget caps |
| GPT-5.5 skeleton-author prompt asset (new) | Create | Geometry-skeleton-author prompt: canvas contract + coordinate system + JSON schema |
| score-lift-per-dollar A/B harness (new) | Create | Measure recovered tiles and $/recovered-tile on 2D holdouts vs deterministic-only |
<!-- /ANCHOR:scope -->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:requirements -->
## 4. REQUIREMENTS

### P0 - Blockers (MUST complete)

| ID | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|---------------------|
| REQ-001 | Escalate ONLY on high-risk 2D tiles that already failed the deterministic skeleton path (incl. best-of-3); never on linear tiles or tiles already passing the gate | In a 45-tile dry run the classifier logs a `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` decision per tile, and GPT-5.5 calls on linear or already-SHIP tiles equal 0 |
| REQ-002 | GPT-5.5 authors a JSON geometry skeleton in the phase-004 schema only - never final tile HTML/CSS | Output validates against the skeleton JSON schema; no HTML/CSS is emitted; the unchanged phase-004 compile + GLM-render path consumes the skeleton |
| REQ-003 | Hard cost cap is enforced before spend | `<=1` GPT-5.5 call per tile and `<=4200` GPT-5.5 tokens per tile; the batch aborts if projected paid calls exceed the `N_2D` count reported before spend; any over-cap call is refused and logged |

### P1 - Required (complete OR user-approved deferral)

| ID | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------------|---------------------|
| REQ-004 | Dispatch uses cli-opencode `opencode run --model openai/gpt-5.5-fast --variant xhigh` with provider auth pre-flight and no silent model substitution | Pre-flight asserts `OPENAI_OK=1`; if openai is not configured the escalation is disabled and the operator is asked - never substituted |
| REQ-005 | Score lift is measured per GPT-5.5 dollar on 2D holdouts vs deterministic-skeleton-only | A/B report on the same failed 2D cases emits recovered-tile count and `$/recovered-tile`; linear tiles are excluded from the metric |
<!-- /ANCHOR:requirements -->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:success-criteria -->
## 5. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- **SC-001**: On the 2D holdout set, the GPT-5.5 escalation recovers at least +3 tiles over deterministic-skeleton-only (the iter-r4-risk go/no-go bar), within the `<=1` call / `<=4200` token per-tile cap. Research predicts +1 to +3 on the hardest 2D cases at confidence 0.78, so a sub-+3 result rejects this phase in favor of downgrade-to-linear.
- **SC-002**: Linear tiles stay flat (within +/-0-2 pts) and consume zero GPT-5.5 tokens, because they are never escalated.
<!-- /ANCHOR:success-criteria -->

| Dependency | Phase 001 gate + phase 002 router | Cannot detect 2D failures to escalate | Hard predecessors; escalation consumes the gate's failure-JSON and the router's 2D classification |

| Risk | GLM rewrites the scaffold geometry | Skeleton effort wasted | Carry the phase-001/004 render contract that forbids editing `data-layout-id`, `top`, `left`, `width`, `height`, and zone styles |

# Implementation Plan: GPT-5.5 Geometry-Skeleton Author (2D-Risk Escalation)

<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 1 -->
<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: plan-core | v2.2 -->

<!--
SELF-CHECK:
- Confirm the plan names the simplest viable approach, affected surfaces, and verification path.
- Match phases to the stated scope; remove setup theater that does not change the outcome.
-->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:summary -->
## 1. SUMMARY

### Technical Context

| Aspect | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Language/Stack** | Node.js ESM (the bento generation harness, `gen-tile.mjs`) |
| **Framework** | None - file-emitting CLI harness; renderer is GLM-5.2 |
| **Storage** | None - tile artifacts + per-tile skeleton/gate JSON on disk |
| **Testing** | Phase-001 deterministic gate + a score-lift-per-dollar A/B holdout harness |
| **Escalation model** | GPT-5.5-fast via cli-opencode (`openai` provider, paid) |
| **Renderer model** | GLM-5.2 via Z.AI Coding Plan (subscription, dispatch cost 0) |

### Overview
When the deterministic skeleton path (phase 004, including its best-of-3 recompute) fails the phase-001 spatial gate on a high-risk 2D tile, dispatch GPT-5.5-fast at `--variant xhigh` to author a fresh coordinate/safe-zone skeleton in the existing phase-004 JSON schema. Phase 004 compiles that skeleton into the same locked HTML/CSS scaffold and GLM-5.2 renders it; the phase-001 gate re-runs. The branch is cost-capped (`<=1` call and `<=4200` tokens per tile, batch budget gated on the pre-spend `N_2D` count) and measured per GPT-5.5 dollar against deterministic-skeleton-only.
<!-- /ANCHOR:summary -->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:quality-gates -->
## 2. QUALITY GATES

### Definition of Ready
- [ ] Phase 004 skeleton service + JSON schema exist and emit/consume the skeleton contract
- [ ] Phase 001 gate emits per-tile failure-JSON; phase 002 router labels tiles linear vs 2D-positioned
- [ ] `openai` provider configured and confirmed via `opencode models openai`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Escalation fires only on residual high-risk 2D failures; zero calls on linear/SHIP tiles
- [ ] Per-tile + batch cost caps enforced and logged
- [ ] Score-lift-per-dollar A/B run on 2D holdouts; go/no-go (>=+3 recovered) recorded
<!-- /ANCHOR:quality-gates -->

---

<!-- ANCHOR:architecture -->
## 3. ARCHITECTURE

### Pattern
Escalation provider behind a deterministic default (strategy + failure-triggered fallback). The deterministic skeleton author stays the default; GPT-5.5 is the escalation author, fired only after deterministic failure on a qualifying tile (AdaCoder adaptive-planning principle, iter-r2-A6).

### Key Components
- **EscalationClassifier**: applies `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` to the tile's primitive, item count, connector needs, and the phase-004 failure state; emits a per-tile decision + reason.
- **Gpt55SkeletonAuthor**: builds the skeleton-author prompt, runs the cli-opencode dispatch, and validates the returned JSON against the phase-004 schema.
- **CostCapAccountant**: enforces `<=1` call and `<=4200` tokens per tile and the batch budget derived from the pre-spend `N_2D`; refuses + logs over-cap calls.
- **SkeletonAuthorRouter**: in `gen-tile.mjs`, routes the 2D skeleton step deterministic-author -> (on failure + escalate) GPT-5.5-author -> (on failure) downgrade-to-linear (phase 004).

### Data Flow
1. Phase 002 labels the tile 2D-positioned; phase 004 computes the deterministic skeleton; GLM renders; phase 001 gate runs.
2. On gate failure, phase 004 best-of-3 recompute runs; if it still fails, control reaches the EscalationClassifier.
3. If `trigger_gpt55_if` holds and `skip_gpt55_if` does not, and the batch budget allows, dispatch GPT-5.5 to author a skeleton.

```bash

PROVIDERS=$(opencode providers list 2>&1)
echo "$PROVIDERS" | grep -q "openai" && OPENAI_OK=1 || OPENAI_OK=0

  --model openai/gpt-5.5-fast \
  --variant xhigh \
  --format json \
  --dir "$REPO_ROOT" \
  "$SKELETON_AUTHOR_PROMPT" </dev/null
# Notes:
#   - openai/gpt-5.5-fast = low-latency Fast variant (OpenAI priority service tier); paid premium provider.
#   - --variant xhigh = max reasoning effort (gpt-5.5 effort range tops out at xhigh).

#   - </dev/null gives stdin EOF so a non-interactive run does not hang.
```

```text
You are the geometry skeleton author for a 480px-high maritime B2B dashboard bento tile.
You do not write final UI code. You produce a prevalidated JSON geometry skeleton that will be
compiled into locked HTML/CSS for GLM-5.2 to render.

Hard canvas contract:
- Tile height is 480px.
- diagramFrame = { x: 24, y: 24, w: "calc(100% - 48px)", h: 312, maxY: 336 }.
- titleFrame   = { x: 24, y: 368, w: "calc(100% - 48px)", h: 88 }.

Coordinate system:
- BBoxes are relative to diagramFrame. x and w use 0..1000 horizontal units scaled to diagramFrame width; y and h are pixels 0..312.
- A bbox is valid only if x >= 0, y >= 0, x + w <= 1000, y + h <= 312.

- Min node gap 16px; min text-to-node gap 12px; at most 4 visible rows (collapse remainder to "+N more"); row height 36-42px; row gap 6-8px.

```

The returned object is the phase-004 skeleton schema: `layoutPrimitive`, `riskFlags`, `zones {diagramFrame, titleFrame}`, `coordinateSystem`, `nodes[]`, `rows[]`, `edges[]`, `overflowPlan`, `textRules`, `validation`.

Source: iter-r2-A6 `trigger_gpt55_if` / `skip_gpt55_if` / `cost_caps`; batch ceiling from iter-r4-risk.

1. **Validation gap — what happens when GPT-5.5's skeleton is itself invalid?** REQ-002 says "validates against the schema," but there's **no recovery path defined for a *spatially* invalid skeleton**. GPT-5.5 will emit bboxes that violate its own `x+w ≤ 1000 / y+h ≤ 312` rule or overlap (RC-2 is exactly this failure mode). The spec assumes schema-valid ⇒ spatially-valid. It does not. **This is the biggest hole**: without a per-node overlap/containment check (the same gate RC-2 needs), a well-formed-but-colliding skeleton silently reaches GLM and re-fails.

2. **Single-shot assumption is optimistic.** Research predicts +1–3 at conf 0.78 — that conf number is for *the approach*, not for *one attempt*. A non-iterative `--variant xhigh` dispatch with no self-critique gives you exactly the "direct numerical positioning → collisions" failure the cited literature warns about. The open question "1 call vs best-of-2" under-states it: the real need is **1 generate + 1 self-verify pass** (GPT checks its own bbox table against the contract before returning), not best-of-2.

3. **Token cap (4200) is likely too small for dense 2D tiles.** A matrix/node-diagram with >8 nodes, edges, pills, and a `validation` block — the listed schema fields (`nodes[]`, `rows[]`, `edges[]`, `overflowPlan`, `validation`) alone at xhigh reasoning effort will burn 2.5–3K of the budget on thinking before emitting JSON. You risk truncated/malformed JSON. No `max_tokens` budgeting or partial-output handling is specified.

4. **No measurement of *why* it fails.** SC-001 only gates on recovered-tile count. If GPT-5.5 recovers <3, you'll know *that* it failed but not *whether* (a) the skeleton was spatially invalid, (b) valid-but-ugly, or (c) valid-but-GLM-mangled-it. Tag each post-escalation failure with `invalid_skeleton | collision_present | glm_rewrite | still_overflow`. Without this, the parent open question (worth it vs downgrade?) is unanswerable.

5. **`diagramFrame h:312 / maxY:336` inconsistency.** `y:24 + h:312 = 336`, but the prompt says "nothing may enter y=336..368" while also "title renders bottom-left in titleFrame (y:368)". That leaves a 32px dead band — intended — but the coordinate system says `y+h ≤ 312` (relative), which is consistent; **however the prompt mixes absolute (`y=336..368`) and relative (`0..312`) framings without a labeled transform**. GPT-5.5 will get this wrong. State the transform explicitly.

6. **RC-3 (title-at-bottom) is under-protected.** The skeleton locks `titleFrame`, but the compile step must *enforce* it; if GLM can still move the title (REQ-risk row says "forbids editing top/left"), that contract lives in phase 001/004 — **verify it actually forbids the title zone, not just diagram nodes.** Unverified assumption.

## Strongest improvement or alternative

**Add a deterministic spatial validator between GPT-5.5 and GLM, and re-escalate once on invalid output — before you build anything else in this phase.**

Concretely: after `Gpt55SkeletonAuthor` returns JSON, run a pure-deterministic checker (`bboxContainment(node, diagramFrame)` + `pairwiseOverlap(nodes)` + `titleZoneClear`) over the skeleton. Three outcomes:
- **Valid** → compile + render (current path).
- **Invalid, budget remains** → one re-dispatch with the violated-rule IDs fed back ("node#3 overlaps node#5 by 18px; fix") — this is cheaper and far more effective than best-of-2-blind.
- **Invalid, no budget** → downgrade-to-linear, log `invalid_skeleton`.

This single addition (a) closes gap #1 (the real RC-2 risk), (b) replaces the weak "1 vs best-of-2" open question with a principled "1 + 1 conditional repair," and (c) makes SC-001 failures diagnosable (gap #4). It costs ~1 deterministic pass + ≤1 extra GPT-5.5 call on the *worst* tiles only — well inside the existing batch budget if you raise the per-tile cap to 2 calls while keeping `≤N_2D + repair_calls` as the batch ceiling. **Make the validator the P0, not an afterthought.**

Secondary: raise the token cap to ~6000 or switch the dispatch to `max_completion_tokens`-bounded structured output, and fix the absolute/relative coordinate framing in the prompt.

## One thing to test or verify before building this phase

**Dry-run the skeleton-author prompt against the 5 lowest-scoring 2D tiles *today* (manually, one `opencode run` per tile) and run the deterministic spatial validator on the raw JSON** — before writing EscalationClassifier/CostCapAccountant. You will learn in one hour: (1) does xhigh emit valid contained bboxes at all, (2) the real token cost, (3) whether the single-shot assumption (gap #2) holds. If ≥3/5 skeletons are spatially invalid on first pass, the validator-and-repair design above is mandatory and the phase's cost model changes. This is the cheapest possible go/no-go and the spec currently defers it to Phase 3.

part.text:
## Verdict
**AGREE-WITH-CHANGES** — confidence 0.72 — The skeleton-author escalation is the right architecture, but the plan over-optimistically assumes a single GPT-5.5 shot will produce *spatially* valid JSON and glosses several dispatch/prompt/cost details that will break on first implementation.

## What GPT-5.5 got right
- **Role boundary**: skeleton-only, never final tile code, keeps GLM-5.2 as renderer (matches iter-r2-A6).
- **Failure-triggered escalation**: deterministic skeleton + best-of-3 first, GPT-5.5 only on residual 2D failures (AdaCoder principle).
- **Linear-tile exclusion**: zero paid spend on already-strong linear primitives.
- **Cost-gating intent**: per-tile cap, batch `N_2D` ceiling, explicit go/no-go on `$/recovered-tile`.
- **Schema reuse**: re-uses phase-004 skeleton schema/compile path, so this phase is a swap of author, not a new pipeline.

## Gaps / risks / errors

1. **Unverified model slug** — The spec dispatches `openai/gpt-5.5-fast --variant xhigh`, but `.opencode/skills/cli-opencode/SKILL.md` only documents `openai/gpt-5.5-pro`; `gpt-5.5-fast` is not listed and may not resolve. **Verify with `opencode models openai` before locking the plan.**

2. **No enforceable 4200-token cap** — The invocation shown has no `--max-tokens` / `max_completion_tokens` flag, so the cap is an accounting target, not a hard limit. Dense matrix/node diagrams with >8 nodes + edges can exceed 4200 tokens at `xhigh` reasoning effort, producing truncated JSON.

3. **Prompt omits the actual tile content** — The skeleton author must know *what* to lay out (the source brief, items, relationships, possibly the reference image). The shown prompt is only canvas/constraint prose. Without the content payload GPT-5.5 cannot author a meaningful skeleton.

4. **Schema-valid ≠ spatially valid** (RC-2) — GPT-5.5 can emit JSON that parses against the schema yet contains overlapping bboxes or content that overflows `diagramFrame`. The spec assumes schema validation is enough; it is not. A deterministic `bboxContainment + pairwiseOverlap + titleZoneClear` validator is required between GPT-5.5 and GLM.

5. **Single-shot assumption is over-optimistic** — Research predicts `+1 to +3` at confidence 0.78 for *the approach*, not for one non-iterative attempt. Hard 2D tiles fail precisely because direct coordinate placement collides; one more direct placement by a stronger model does not magically solve collision avoidance without a verification loop.

6. **No failure taxonomy for SC-001** — If recovery is `<+3`, the spec does not tell you *why*: `invalid_skeleton`, `collision_present`, `glm_rewrote_scaffold`, `still_overflow`, `downgraded`. Without tags, the parent open question (worth it vs downgrade?) is unanswerable.

7. **Trigger/skip priority is ambiguous** — `trigger_gpt55_if` lists broad primitives (`matrix, flow, hub, branch, popover, node-diagram, network, connector-map`) while `skip_gpt55_if` lists exemptions. The spec must state explicitly that **skip rules override trigger rules**, otherwise a simple 3-item flow could still escalate.

8. **GLM scaffold compliance is assumed, not enforced** — The mitigation is a prose contract forbidding edits to `data-layout-id`, `top`, `left`, etc. After GLM render, a deterministic DOM check must verify those exact attributes/values are preserved; otherwise the skeleton effort is wasted (RC-1/RC-3 can return).

9. **Statistical noise on +3 bar** — One tile is ~2.2 percentage points on n=45. A `+3` recovery claim is within sampling noise unless measured as paired tile-level deltas with a holdout set, not a single batch run.

10. **`$/recovered-tile` denominator can be zero** — If GPT-5.5 recovers 0 tiles, the metric is undefined. The spec needs a break-even value model or a floor reporting rule.

11. **A6 is a derivative bet** — The research says implement A5/004 skeleton-first first and keep A6 as an escalation. The spec treats A6 as a fixed phase 5, but it only pays off if A5 leaves dense 2D failures that are worth paid recovery. Consider making A6 contingent on A5's measured residual.

12. **No temperature/reproducibility setting** — A geometry solver should run at low temperature or structured output; the spec omits this.

## Strongest improvement or alternative
**Insert a deterministic spatial validator after GPT-5.5 and allow one conditional repair dispatch, not a blind best-of-2.** Concretely:
1. GPT-5.5 emits skeleton JSON.
2. Deterministic validator checks `bboxContainment`, `pairwiseOverlap`, `titleZoneClear`, `rowCap`.
3. If invalid **and** budget remains, re-dispatch GPT-5.5 with the violated rule IDs and offending node IDs ("node#3 overlaps node#5 by 18px; fix").
4. If still invalid or no budget, log `invalid_skeleton` and downgrade-to-linear.

This closes the real RC-2 risk, replaces the weak "1 vs best-of-2" open question with a principled verify-then-repair loop, and makes SC-001 failures diagnosable. Raise the per-tile cap to `<=2` calls but keep the batch ceiling tight.

## One thing to test or verify before building this phase
**Manually dry-run the skeleton-author prompt on the 5 lowest-scoring 2D tiles today** with the actual tile brief/image included, then run a deterministic spatial validator on the returned JSON. Measure: (a) whether `openai/gpt-5.5-fast` resolves and real token count, (b) schema validity, (c) spatial validity, (d) how many collisions GPT-5.5 produces in one shot. If ≥3 of 5 skeletons are spatially invalid on first pass, the validator-and-repair design above is mandatory and the cost model changes before you write the classifier or accountant.
